            | where("user_id"_t1 < "user_id"_t2);

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace mathematical_operations_analysis {

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  std::string name;
  double price;
  int quantity;
  double discount_rate;
};

struct MathAnalysis {
  int product_id;
  std::string name;
  double abs_price;
  double ceil_price;
  double floor_price;
  double rounded_price;
  double exp_discount;
  double ln_price;
  double log2_quantity;
  double sin_discount;
  double cos_discount;
  double tan_discount;
  double sqrt_quantity;
};

std::vector<MathAnalysis> analyze_mathematical_operations(const std::vector<Product>& products) {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;
  
  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();
  
  // Insert products into the database
  sqlgen::write(conn, products).value();
  
  // Build the query with mathematical operations
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<Product>(
    "product_id"_c,
    "name"_c,
    sqlgen::abs("price"_c).as<"abs_price">(),
    sqlgen::ceil("price"_c).as<"ceil_price">(),
    sqlgen::floor("price"_c).as<"floor_price">(),
    sqlgen::round("price"_c, 2).as<"rounded_price">(),
    sqlgen::exp("discount_rate"_c / 100).as<"exp_discount">(),
    sqlgen::ln("price"_c).as<"ln_price">(),
    sqlgen::log2("quantity"_c).as<"log2_quantity">(),
    sqlgen::sin("discount_rate"_c).as<"sin_discount">(),
    sqlgen::cos("discount_rate"_c).as<"cos_discount">(),
    sqlgen::tan("discount_rate"_c).as<"tan_discount">(),
    sqlgen::sqrt("quantity"_c).as<"sqrt_quantity">()
  ) | sqlgen::to<std::vector<MathAnalysis>>;
  
  // Execute the query and return results
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace mathematical_operations_analysis
```</answer>

  std::string display_name;
  std::optional<std::string> bio;
  std::optional<std::string> avatar_url;
};

struct Result {
  int profile_id;
  std::string display_name;
};

std::vector<Result> column_is_null_checks() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Connect to the database
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite").value();

  // Create a read operation for Profile with the conditions
  auto read_op = sqlgen::read<std::vector<Profile>> 
               | sqlgen::where("bio"_c.is_null() && "avatar_url"_c.is_not_null());

  // Execute the query
  auto profiles = read_op(conn).value();

  // Map the results
  std::vector<Result> results;
  for (const auto& profile : profiles) {
    results.push_back(Result{
        .profile_id = profile.profile_id,
        .display_name = profile.display_name
    });
  }

  return results;
}

}  // namespace column_is_null_checks
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>

namespace full_join_suppliers_products {

struct Supplier {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct Product {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> product_id;
  int supplier_id;
  std::string name;
};

struct SupProd {
  std::string supplier_name;
  std::optional<std::string> product_name;
};

/**
 * Returns a vector of SupProd structs, where each struct contains the name of a supplier and the name of a product, if any.
 */
std::vector<SupProd> full_join_suppliers_products() {
  using namespace sqlgen::literals;

  // Create a join query between Supplier and Product with FULL JOIN
  auto join_query = sqlgen::select_from<Supplier, "t1">()
                    | sqlgen::full_join<Product, "t2">("supplier_id"_t1 == "supplier_id"_t2);

  // Select the required columns with appropriate aliases
  auto query = sqlgen::select_from<"j">(join_query, 
                                        "name"_t1.as<"supplier_name">(), 
                                        "name"_t2.as<"product_name">())
               | sqlgen::to<std::vector<SupProd>>();

  // Connect to the database and execute the query
  auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
  return query(conn).value();
}

}  // namespace full_join_suppliers_products
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <sqlgen.hpp>
#include <sqlgen/sqlite.hpp>
#include <vector>

namespace create_view_order_summary {

struct Order {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
};

struct OrderItem {
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> item_id;
  int order_id;
  double unit_price;
  int quantity;
};

struct OrderSummary {
  static constexpr const char* tablename = "OrderSummaryView";
  static constexpr bool is_view = true;
  sqlgen::PrimaryKey<int> order_id;
  std::string customer;
  double total;
};

/**
 * Description: This function demonstrates creating a view.
 * Returns: std::vector<OrderSummary>.
 */
std::vector<OrderSummary> create_view_order_summary() {
    // Connect to the SQLite database
    auto conn = sqlgen::sqlite::connect("test_db.sqlite");
    
    // Create the view using raw SQL since sqlgen's fluent API may not support complex joins/aggregates in the reference materials
    conn->execute(
        "CREATE VIEW OrderSummaryView AS "
        "SELECT o.order_id, o.customer, SUM(oi.unit_price * oi.quantity) as total "
        "FROM Order o "
        "INNER JOIN OrderItem oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id "
        "GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer"
    );
    
    // Read from the view using sqlgen's select_from
    using namespace sqlgen::literals;
    auto query = sqlgen::select_from<OrderSummary>("order_id"_c, "customer"_c, "total"_c) 
                 | sqlgen::to<std::vector<OrderSummary>>;
    
    auto result = query(conn);
    
    if (result.is_ok()) {
        return result.value();
    } else {
        throw std::runtime_error(result.error().what());
    }
}

}  // namespace create_view_order_summary
```</answer>
